title: astragalus peruvianus
intro: Astragalus peruvianus, also known as Peruvian milk vetch, is a flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its adaptogenic and immunomodulatory properties, which enable it to stimulate the body's natural response to stress and support immune function. The medicinal properties of Astragalus peruvianus are attributed to its rich content of saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced antioxidant defenses. Astragalus peruvianus can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as capsules, teas, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
